content: PEP 544 Protocol = structural typing: any class with the right methods satisfies it, NO inheritance/registration needed (unlike ABC). Ideal for DI seams where the module must not import app code — module defines Protocol, app provides a duck-typed impl. @runtime_checkable only verifies member PRESENCE not signatures. Best practice: keep protocols minimal; use Protocol for seams crossing the module/app boundary, ABC only when you want a shared base impl + enforced subclassing inside one codebase.
